summary: DHH explains why 37signals moved their podcast to Buzzsprout, a decision driven not by features or price but by a personal connection with cofounder Tom Rossi from the Rails community. He reflects on how modern commerce is usually impersonal, but when you can connect with the person running a business, it changes the buying dynamic entirely. He draws a parallel to choosing Braintree fifteen years ago because of a personal connection with CEO Bryan Johnson. The post argues that this ability to sell through personal relationships is a unique competitive advantage for small and medium-sized businesses, especially in commodity markets.
key_points: 37signals chose Buzzsprout for podcast hosting based on personal connection with its cofounder, not features or price
In-person encounters at events like Rails World can forge business relationships that transcend commodity comparisons
Small and medium businesses have a unique advantage in cultivating personal connections that large companies cannot replicate
Buying decisions can be a form of voting with your wallet for the kind of businesses you want to see succeed
money_quotes: Podcast hosting is to some extent a commodity market, so this was less about pining for a specific feature or even working to reduce the bill. This was about buying from Tom Rossi.
This in-person encounter made me reflect on modern commerce. How impersonal it is most of the time.
I usually would rather deal with a web form than a salesperson, but it's different when you can establish a connection to someone actually running the show.
He made it feel like we were buying from Bryan, more so than buying from Braintree.
You can far more easily cultivate and maintain these personal connections. Make the exceptions when that's needed. Be available when it matters.
By doing so, you allow your customers to buy the seller rather than just the product or service. That's a powerful advantage, especially in commodity markets.
This also represents an opportunity for buyers to directly vote with their wallet for what they'd like to see more of in this world.
key_insight: In commodity markets, the most powerful differentiator for small businesses is the personal connection between buyer and seller — people buy people, not just products.
